name: Caretos of Podence (Entrudo Chocalheiro)
description: A UNESCO-recognized tradition featuring masked figures in colorful fringed suits and brass bells who perform ritual dances and tease spectators.

name: Carnival in Torres Vedras
description: Known as Portugal’s Most Portuguese Carnival, it features political satire, large caricature heads (cabeçudos), and 'matrafonas' (men dressed as women).

name: Carnival in Ovar
description: Noted for artistic costume design and samba schools, including the 'Noite Mágica' (Magic Night) all-night street celebration.

name: Carnival in Loulé
description: The Algarve's premier carnival merging Brazilian-style samba schools with traditional elements and elaborate moving float sculptures.

name: Carnival in Lisbon
description: A modern take on traditions with parades in Parque das Nações, community events in Alfama, and various themed parties.

name: Carnival in Porto
description: Combines traditional elements with art installations and street performances, featuring unique carnival boat parades along the Douro River.

name: Carnival in Famalicão
description: A festival known for preserving local traditions and modern elements, particularly famous for its large children’s parade.

name: Carnival in Mealhada
description: Known as Carnival City, it features authentic Portuguese samba schools and spectacular performances.
